title: Destructure and Display
description: Use destructuring to extract data from an object and print a formatted message.
problemStatement:
You have an object representing a book:

```javascript
const book = {
  title: 'The Great Gatsby',
  author: 'F. Scott Fitzgerald',
  year: 1925,
  publisher: 'Scribner'
};
```

Write a function `displayBookInfo` that takes this object and prints a summary string using template literals. The function should destructure the object in its parameter list. The summary should be:

`"[title] by [author] was published in [year] by [publisher]."`

If `publisher` is missing, default to `'unknown'`.

Also, create an array of the book's values (title, author, year, publisher) using the spread operator and log that array.
hints: Destructure in the function parameter: `function displayBookInfo({ title, author, year, publisher = 'unknown' })`
Use template literal to build the string.

For the array, you can create an array and spread the object values? Actually object values aren't iterable directly, but you can do `Object.values(book)` or manually create an array with the variables. The problem suggests using spread, so maybe create an array literal with spread of the object's values: `[...Object.values(book)]`? But that's fine.
expectedOutput:
The Great Gatsby by F. Scott Fitzgerald was published in 1925 by Scribner.
[ 'The Great Gatsby', 'F. Scott Fitzgerald', 1925, 'Scribner' ]
